heading: Encumbrances on lands allotted to applicants for enrollment in Five Civilized Tribes; use of interest on tribal funds
label: 86 Encumbrances on lands allotted to applicants for enrollment in Five Civilized Tribes; use of interest on tribal funds
text: Land allotted to any applicant for enrollment as a citizen in the Five Civilized Tribes whether an Indian or freedman, shall not be affected or encumbered by any deed, debt, or obligation of any character contracted prior to the time at which said land may be alienated under the laws of the United States: , That the interest accruing from tribal funds and deposited in banks in the State of Oklahoma may be used as authorized by the Act of March third, nineteen hundred and eleven, under the direction of the Secretary of the Interior, to defray the expense of per capita payments authorized by Congress.Provided further
notes: References in TextAct of March third, nineteen hundred and eleven, referred to in text, is , , which, insofar as classified to the Code, enacted sections 11, 118, 143, 156, 300, and 301 of this title. For classification of this Act to the Code, see Tables.act Mar. 3, 1911, ch. 21036 Stat. 1060
